# Write the first draft

Use `brief.md` and `research.md`. Return a complete `draft.md`, not an outline.

If the grounding outcome is `ungrounded`, do not manufacture an article.
Instead write a clearly labeled non-publishable draft that explains the
unsupported premise, preserves any supported material, and proposes the
smallest research needed to continue.

Otherwise:

- serve the stated audience and single takeaway;
- use only supported claims, preserving needed qualifications;
- attach unobtrusive source references to every material factual claim;
- distinguish reported fact, analysis, and opinion;
- write specific prose with a purposeful opening, coherent progression, and
  earned ending;
- preserve the requested voice without filler or synthetic quotations.

End with `<!-- COMPLETE -->` when every factual claim can be traced through the
source-to-claim map.
